head.title: Frequently Asked Questions | Compostable Coffee Capsules | Coffee Bio Caps

faq.intro:
At Coffee Bio Caps, we want to ensure you have all the information you need before making the switch to a more sustainable coffee service.
Here, we answer the most frequently asked questions about our compostable and biodegradable coffee capsules, our coffee capsule manufacturing service, the hire of Nespresso-compatible machines, and the benefits for hotels, offices and brands.

question1.question: Are your capsules really compostable?
question1.answer: Yes, our capsules are 100% compostable and biodegradable. They break down completely within 3-6 months in industrial composting facilities and can be disposed of with organic waste.

question2.question: Are your capsules compatible with Nespresso machines?
question2.answer: Yes, all our capsules are designed to be fully compatible with standard Nespresso machines.

question3.question: What is your minimum order quantity?
question3.answer: We work with businesses of all sizes. Please contact us directly to discuss your specific requirements.

question4.question: Do you offer bulk discounts?
question4.answer: Yes, we offer competitive pricing for bulk orders. Contact us for a custom quotation.

question5.question: What is your delivery timeframe?
question5.answer: Delivery times vary depending on location and order size. We typically deliver within 5-10 business days.

question6.question: Can we customize the packaging with our brand?
question6.answer: Yes, we offer custom branding options for our capsules and packaging. Contact our team for details.

question7.question: What coffee origins do you offer?
question7.answer: We source premium coffee from Colombia, Brazil, Guatemala, Ethiopia, and other sustainable suppliers.

question8.question: Do you provide technical support for the machines?
question8.answer: Yes, our machine rental service includes full technical support and maintenance.

question9.question: What is the shelf life of Coffee Caps compostable household capsules, and how does this differ from plastic and aluminium capsules?
question9.answer: The shelf life of Coffee Caps capsules is 2 years from the date of manufacture, which is similar to that of aluminium and plastic capsules. Please note that this is not the shelf life of the end product, which will be different.

question10.question: How do Coffee Caps home-compostable capsules differ from other compostable capsules on the market?
question10.answer: An important feature of Coffee Caps is that they are certified as home-compostable and 100% organic (derived from living matter). You can put your capsule in your home compost and it will break down completely within 6 months.

question11.question: Why are Coffee Caps home-compostable capsules better than recyclable aluminium capsules?
question11.answer: Turning bauxite into aluminium requires enormous amounts of energy, and then turning recyclable aluminium back into capsules again involves a significant carbon footprint. Aluminium has well-documented environmental issues, affecting both aquatic and terrestrial ecosystems. By using Coffee Caps compostable capsules at home, you are directly reducing your household waste in the most environmentally friendly way possible: from nature to nature.

question12.question: Which consumer machines are the Coffee Caps compostable capsules compatible with?
question12.answer: Our Coffee Caps are compatible with all Nespresso machines manufactured since 2010.

question13.question: What are Coffee Caps made of?
question13.answer: Coffee Caps are made from biopolymers and organic material.

question14.question: What percentage of the capsule material comes from sustainable sources?
question14.answer: Coffee Caps are 100% bio-based and come entirely from sustainable sources. Coffee Caps are made from biopolymers and organic material.

question15.question: Do Coffee Caps contain any fossil-based plastics, BPA or BPA derivatives, or PFCs?
question15.answer: Coffee Caps do not contain any petroleum-based plastics, nor do they contain BPA or PFCs.

question17.question: What certifications do Coffee Caps have?
question17.answer: Coffee Caps have a 4-star OK Biobased rating from the notified body TÜV Austria. This is the highest possible quality rating.

question18.question: Are Coffee Caps biodegradable?
question18.answer: Yes, Coffee Caps are biodegradable through home composting.

question19.question: Are Coffee Caps compostable at home?
question19.answer: Yes, Coffee Caps are compostable at home.

question20.question: What is the difference between industrially compostable and home-compostable?
question20.answer: Industrial composting plants handle very large quantities, ranging from 2,000 to 10,000 tonnes. Being certified as “compostable” through an industrial plant means that the material will break down within 3 months due to high temperatures and biological activity. In home composting, the temperature and humidity are lower because there is less organic waste. Products certified as home compostable take up to 6 months to break down.

question21.question: What is the best way to dispose of Coffee Caps' compostable capsules at home?
question21.answer: The best solution is to put your Coffee Caps in your home compost or community compost bin.

question22.question: Can Coffee Caps' home-compostable capsules be put in the organic waste bin?
question22.answer: Yes, Coffee Caps can be placed in your home compost or community compost bin.

faq.final_message: Do you have any further questions about our compostable coffee capsules or our services?
faq.final_message_contact: Get in touch and one of our specialist advisors will help you design the perfect solution for your business.
